scope.summary: Build the first premium, responsive resume and project portfolio landing page from the approved resume content and public-safe assets, ready for GitHub Pages.
exclusions: No framework, bundler, backend, database, analytics, tracking, paid service, custom domain, or image generation
No private or unsanitized client material
No claim that the current public Skin Element site is the archived Shopify rebuild
definitionOfDone: Semantic index.html, styles.css, and minimal script.js implement the full site without a build system
Approved titles, dates, metrics, links, and claims remain accurate
Hero, summary, toolkit, skills, experience, projects, education, contact, and PDF download are present
The Skin Element project is labeled as an unreleased archived Shopify rebuild
Responsive behavior is verified at 1440px, 768px, and 375px with no horizontal overflow
Accessibility, link, asset, metadata, print, reduced-motion, and console checks pass
GitHub Pages project-path compatibility is verified
Verification evidence is recorded in the pull request and repository state files

implementation.assetStrategy: No approved project imagery was supplied; v1 uses the approved premium text-led direction.

commands.evidence: Exited 0 with no output after the final implementation changes.
Local preview served at http://127.0.0.1:4173/; index, styles.css, script.js, and PDF returned HTTP 200.
111 authoritative source fragments and all 15 source links were found; required relative asset references and archived-project language were present.
HTML validation completed with 0 errors and 0 warnings after the final markup fixes.
Both JavaScript files passed syntax checks.

commands.command: Playwright 1.61.1 Chromium assertion run at 1440x1000, 768x1024, and 375x812
commands.evidence: All three viewports reported 0 overflow elements, 0 text outside the viewport, one H1, 19 ordered headings, 0 console warnings/errors, 0 page errors, and 0 failed local requests.
Full-page CLI screenshots completed at 1440x1000, 768x1024, and 375x812 and were visually reviewed for overlap, clipping, hierarchy, and responsive behavior.

commands.command: GitHub Pages project-path simulation at /keanan-wilson-resume/ on temporary port 4174
commands.evidence: Page, stylesheet, script, and PDF returned HTTP 200 under the project prefix; resolved asset and download URLs retained the prefix; 375px document width remained 375px; console and request-failure logs were empty.

manualChecks: Keyboard Tab order begins with the visible skip link; skip activation transfers focus to Summary; all sampled focus outlines were solid and 3px.
Mobile menu opens, reports aria-expanded=true, closes on Escape, and returns focus to the Menu button.
Internal navigation lands below the sticky header; the PDF download event produced keanan-wilson-resume.pdf.
Sampled text contrast ranged from 5.88:1 to 14.73:1; reduced-motion media changed smooth scrolling to auto and reduced transitions to 0.01ms.
Print media rendered as an eight-page Letter PDF with white backgrounds, black text, hidden navigation/footer controls, and no clipped or orphaned final content.
The approved PDF is a four-page PDF 1.4 file with SHA-256 e86a5b4d27a6ecad78401426823acd168e21b54defb6b736fb9757cd0e7e46d6.
No images are included because no approved public imagery was supplied; image-alt verification is not applicable for v1.
limitations: Automated external-link requests returned HTTP 200 for 5 authoritative destinations and HTTP 403 for 10 brand or Upwork destinations; no checked URL returned 404 or 410. The exact approved source links remain unchanged.
The feature branch is not merged and GitHub Pages is not yet launched, so the canonical production URL has not been verified against a live deployment.

contextProjection.reason: A new public resume and portfolio site changes Keanan's public professional presence and adds a maintained LafaYeti repository.
evidence: CU1 is implemented and verified on feature/resume-landing-page-v1, but no merge or final public URL exists yet.
Cross-repository projection remains deferred until the reviewed pull request is merged and the GitHub Pages URL is confirmed.
